checks.finding: design.md ## Architecture mermaid names top-level component boundaries and integration edges into existing components (hitl-loop.template.sh, check-placeholders.sh/.ps1, tests/lib/loop-driver.*, loop-driver.tests.ps1, loop-consistency.tests.ps1, run-all.sh/.ps1, test.yml); ## Components Technology column + ## Layer Specifications table cover tech choices per applicable layer (Bash/PowerShell scripts, GitHub Actions YAML; Frontend/UX explicitly N/A, matching ux-spec.md/frontend-spec.md).
Directed edges in design.md ## Architecture (HWT->HLT/WFISKILL/WFIDOCS; SEED->CPB->CP; SEED->LC->LD; DP/SP->GPA; DP/SP-.->LDR->LDT/LCT; RA->HWT&CPB) form a DAG; no cycle found.

design.md ## Data Plan carries all three required sub-fields with explicit content: 'Data Entities:' (new fixture dir + mktemp fixtures + two read-only real WFI files), 'Existing Data Affected: none.', 'Migration Strategy: none.'

design.md ## API / Contract Plan documents every new script/suite contract (HITL driving technique, assert_wfi_audit_transition reference-check contract, brownfield seed layout, the brownfield lock suite's two-case contract, loop-consistency brownfield leg, and full param() surfaces for the two new precheck .ps1 ports), the CLI-contract equivalent of endpoint coverage for this non-HTTP feature; round-2 addition confirmed: pwd -P normalization (INV-030/AC-018) and jq non-use (INV-031/AC-018) now explicit in the HITL/WFI-audit leg steps.

design.md ## Security Boundaries table names 4 trust boundaries (B1-B4) with Auth/Authz Mechanism, Data Classification (no PII; internal/synthetic data only), and OWASP Concerns columns; security-spec.md elaborates with a full STRIDE table and OWASP mapping.

SKIP: Feature Type is 'deterministic test infrastructure + two precheck script ports (Pillar A completion)' - non-fullstack. design.md has no ## Frontend Plan or ## Backend Plan sections; ux-spec.md and frontend-spec.md both declare N/A with no GUI/browser surface. Matches round-1's own SKIP for this check.

design.md ## Test Strategy covers unit-level red/green pairs (item 1-2), integration/self-healing observables against existing suites (item 3), runtime-budget verification (item 4), self-registration checks (item 5), and concrete named commands (bash tests/run-all.sh, pwsh tests/run-all.ps1, 3-OS CI matrix - item 6); acceptance-tests.md maps every AC-NNN to a TEST-NNN with concrete assertions.

Every Architecture-diagram node is either a ## Components table row, described in the 'Real surfaces exercised READ-ONLY' paragraph (hitl-loop.template.sh, check-placeholders.sh/.ps1, tests/lib/loop-driver.sh/.ps1, wfi-audit-cycle/SKILL.md), or an established pre-existing system component identifiable from investigation.md (tests/loop-driver.tests.ps1, tests/loop-consistency.tests.ps1 - INV-013/014/021).

design.md ## ADR Change Log states 'No new ADR' and cites docs/adr/0010-loop-inventory-and-fixture-vocabulary.md:46-47; verified this file exists at that path and lines 46-47 contain the cited greenfield/brownfield vocabulary text. No undeclared new architectural decision identified.
